# Reconcile — Usage Guide

*Mode: direct · 🔌 offline (no LLM) · requires: qpdf*

Part II of the KR Choksey → GnuCash flow. Matches the contract notes (SLBM memos and trade notes) against the Part I Simplified Ledger and produces a "Bills" workbook. Runs fully offline (deterministic, no LLM).

## When to use it

Run this second, after "KRChoksey" (Part I). Its Bills workbook is the input to "GnuCash Import" (Part III).

## Inputs

- **Part I Simplified Ledger workbook** (required) — accepts: Excel (.xlsx) matching *-KRC-Ledger.xlsx.
  - The Part I Simplified Ledger — pick it from the list of prior outputs.
  - ⚠️ Run Part I first; the picker only lists Ledger workbooks from earlier runs.
- **Contract Notes (SLBM memos + trade notes)** (required) — accepts: PDF (.pdf) — one or many contract notes.
  - The contract-note PDFs — SLBM memos plus trade notes.
  - ⚠️ Include every contract note for the period so the reconciliation is complete.
- **PDF password (account holder's PAN)** (required) — accepts: Text — the account holder's PAN.
  - The PDF password — the account holder's PAN.
  - ⚠️ Decryption uses qpdf (bundled in the frozen build).

## How to run

1. Pick the Part I Simplified Ledger from the dropdown.
2. Select all the contract-note PDFs.
3. Enter the PDF password (PAN).
4. Click Run and download the Bills workbook.

## Output

Output is written to the **`Data/outputs/`** folder, named:

```
Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-KRC-Bills-Recon.xlsx
```

Files produced:

- **`YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-KRC-Bills-Recon.xlsx`** — The reconciled Bills workbook tying contract notes to the ledger. This is the file you pick in the GnuCash Import (Part III) tab.

## Tips

A complete set of contract notes is what makes Part II tie out — missing notes show up as unmatched bills.

## Troubleshooting

| If… | Then… |
|------|-------|
| Some bills are unmatched. | A contract note is probably missing for the period. Add it and re-run. |
| PDF won't open. | Check the PAN password and that qpdf is available in source mode. |
